author: 本人不在😉
content:
[关于抖音的一些反馈仅供参考] 1.关于抖音推送问题：抖音会反复推送一些我点过不感兴趣的内容，然后进行关键词屏蔽也没有用。
2.关于抖音审核问题：抖音关于营销号的审核以及对于评论的审核不合理。第一营销号的很多内容为恶意剪辑曲解，目的为获取流量，很多不明真相的人会跟风发表一些不正当言论
3.抖音在搜索词推送问题：我主动多次搜索并且已经关注本人为粉丝的情况下会推很多恶意p图，恶意剪辑，网暴他人的引导性言论。
综上：抖音的很多行为具有引导性，不当性，不符合清朗行动的要求，审核应加大审核力度。

author: nfreeze17
content:
[Use it for the Creatives, Not the Platform] YouTube offers creative individuals a space to share their videos, and those seeking entertainment a place to watch. It has been a staple of the internet since the mid 2000s. YouTube seems to stand tall over competitors because of how long it has been around, and because new creatives are more likely to use an established, popular platform. All of this is to say the YouTube sucks creatives dry and takes advantage of people just trying to watch.

“Content” is the new buzzword. What happened to art, or simply videos? It’s not that those aren’t present, it just seems that creatives are incentivized to make as many videos as possible to build a following or become popular. In some cases, there are creatives who get anywhere from thousands to millions of views, and yet YouTube refuses to pay them a living wage.

What about everyday users? Unfortunately, YouTube as a platform continues to get worse and far more predatory. Users are bombarded with ads when looking for something to watch. In many cases they’re given back to back ads, with many interruptions during the video. Recently, YouTube added pop up ads that display after watching 20+ seconds of ads. Additionally, they’ve hidden how long the ads will be. At this point, it’s about giving YouTube that sweet sweet ad revenue—very little even going to the creators. 

Tired of the ads? You can buy YouTube premium for a monthly price tag higher than most other streaming platforms. YouTube may offer an endless stream of videos uploaded by millions of users, but in many cases these videos don’t have the same production quality of multimillion budget tv shows or movies. In the end, users like myself come to the platform for the creatives, NOT because the platform is great. If there was another platform that offered real competition, I’d gladly jump ship.

author: i国际法途径
content: [腾讯视频平台沦为资本温床，算法偏见扼杀精品！] 原以为腾讯视频深耕内容，现在看来不过是资本的私人后花园。平台对于《白日提灯》的打压简直到了不加掩饰的地步，曝光量甚至不如一些早已“入土”的旧剧。作为付费会员，我极其失望。腾讯视频正亲手毁掉观众对“精品剧”的信任。面对优质作品《白日提灯》，平台公然玩弄算法歧视，固屏缺失、曝光归零，甚至连正常剧的基本宣发待遇都吝啬给予《白日提灯》。相反，平台却对特定资方和艺人的平庸之作极尽谄媚，强行喂饭，这已经不是普通的推流，这是赤裸裸的“劣币驱逐良币”。如果一个平台丧失了基本的公正与专业眼光，只甘心做资本的传声筒和偏心眼的“奶妈””，那么请记住：观众的订阅费是投给好作品的，不是给资本弃儿当垫脚石的。如此吃相，必将被用户抛弃！看着平台不遗余力地给某嘉行艺人的待播及某某完播剧叠Buff、站台，这种“嫡庶有别”的嘴脸实在令人作呕。放着高质量、高口碑的潜力股《白日提灯》不去推广，反而沉迷于这种资本内部的利益置换，你们是在做影视，还是在玩现实版“甄嬛传”？建议平台改名“资本投喂站”别再打着“推荐好剧”的旗号寒了万千剧粉的心！
context: 腾讯视频 App Store评论

author: HeyAngel999
content:
[Snapchat..buggy but fun] I’ve been using Snapchat for over a decade, and it truly stands out as the most fun and engaging app out there. It’s not just about pictures and messaging; when used creatively, it transforms into a game-like experience. With endless customization, fun filters, and the ability to transform your look, Snapchat makes creating content an absolute blast.

However, it’s not without its flaws. Over the years, I’ve noticed persistent bugs—one major issue being the view count not reflecting the actual reach. For instance since December 16th, I’ve had stories that clearly hit thousands and even tens of thousands of views, but the app only shows a fraction of that. It’s frustrating and definitely stifles creativity.

Despite these bugs, I’m still a huge fan. Snapchat remains my go-to for messaging, sharing moments, and unleashing social creativity. I’ll always keep it installed on my devices!
